title: Odisha on heat alert; thunderstorms likely till April 4 - orissapost.com
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Odisha
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Meteorological Department has issued a heat and humidity warning for Odisha, with thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and winds of 30–40 kmph likely in five districts Thursday. Such conditions are expected to persist until April 4.
A yellow warning for heat has been issued for Ganjam, Gajapati and Puri districts, while Nuapada and Nabarangpur are under a yellow warning for thunderstorms.
Weather conditions are likely to change from April 5 onward.
On Wednesday, temperatures crossed 36°C at 24 locations across the state, with Titlagarh recording the highest at 39.5°C. Dhenkanal reported high humidity levels.
Temperatures are expected to rise by 4–5°C over the next two days. The Regional Meteorological Centre has issued a yellow warning for intense heat and humidity in Puri, Ganjam and Gajapati districts until April 4.
